name: Canary Release
def: Rolling a change out to a small subset of traffic first, watching metrics before full rollout.
problem: Limit the blast radius of a bad deploy by validating it on real but limited traffic.
mechanism: Route a small % of traffic to the new version; compare health/error/latency metrics against baseline, then progressively expand or roll back automatically.
benefits: Limits blast radius of bad releases
failures: Canary too small to be statistically meaningful
whenNot: Tiny user bases; non-critical internal tools
myths: "Canary = blue-green" — canary shifts a fraction gradually; blue-green flips between two full environments
